title: What is the N+1 problem in Hibernate, and how can it be mitigated in Spring Data JPA?

answers.junior: The N+1 problem happens when one query loads a list, and then N more queries load related data for each item. It slows down performance.
answers.middle: N+1 occurs when fetching a collection causes additional queries for each item. Use fetch joins or entity graphs to avoid it. Always monitor query logs.
answers.senior: Mitigate N+1 by using fetch joins, entity graphs, or batch fetching. Analyze query plans and optimize relationships. Spring Data JPA supports @EntityGraph and custom queries to address this issue.
answers.tricky: Ignoring N+1 can lead to severe performance degradation. Use profiling tools to detect it. Advanced mitigation includes DTO projections and query optimization. Always validate query performance in production.
